name: Lateral Deltoid (Side Delt)

landmarks.notes: Receives little effective indirect work, tolerates high direct volume, and is a common priority for width. The 24-26 MRV is a model-based upper-bound estimate, not a directly measured ceiling — high-volume dose-response data are sparse. The cited Enes 2024 set-progression trial is on the lower limb (quadriceps), not this muscle, and even there found no significant hypertrophy difference between constant and progressively higher-volume training (only a possible small benefit at higher volumes, held with low certainty). Treat this ceiling as uncertain and individual.

frequency_notes: Responds well to high frequency (2-4x/week) of lateral raise variations.
training_notes: Lateral raises (dumbbell and cable) are the staple. The top of the raise adds little tension; cables/lean-away variations load the lengthened position better.
